title: Division with groups of objects
description: Use visual models to understand division.
topic_path: Module 1: Properties of multiplication and division and solving problems with units of 2–5 and 10
Topic B: Division as an unknown factor problem

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which image could represent $12 \div 6?$**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There must be a total of $\maroonD{12}$ flowers.  

The $\maroonD{12}$ flowers must either be in $\greenD{6 \text{ equal-sized groups}}$ or have $\blueD{6 \text{ flowers in every group}}$.
This image has $\maroonD{12}$ flowers in $\greenD{6\text{ equal-sized groups}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]

Here is  the correct image:


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which expressions could represent the picture shown below?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueD{18}$ whales.  

We can look at $\greenD{\text{how many equal-sized groups of whales}}$  there are or $\maroonD{\text{how many whales are in every group}}$.

### $\greenD{\text{Number of equal-sized groups}}$

There are $\blueD{18}$ total whales divided into $\greenD6$ equal groups.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]
$\blueD{18}$ divided into $\greenD6$ equal groups is $\blueD{18}\div \greenD6$.

### $\maroonD{\text{Number of whales in each group}}$


There are $\blueD{18}$ total whales divided into groups of $\maroonD3$ whales.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

[[☃ image 4]]

[[☃ image 5]]

[[☃ image 6]]
$\blueD{18}$ divided into groups of $\maroonD3$ is $\blueD{18}\div \maroonD3$.

The following expressions could represent the picture:

-  $\blueD{18}\div \greenD6$ 
- $\blueD{18}\div \maroonD3$

options.alt: A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.
A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.
A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.
A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.
A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.
A group of 3 whales has a rectangle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $6$ eggs divided evenly in $3$ baskets.

**Which image could represent this situation?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or 

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$

We have a $\greenD{\text{total}}$ of $\greenD6$ eggs.

The $\blueD{\text{number of groups}}$ of eggs is $\blueD3$.  

Which image shows $\greenD{6}$ eggs divided  evenly in $\blueD{3}$ groups?

This image represents the situation:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![Two groups of 6 eggs. Each group of eggs has a circle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aba3133187fd3834bbe30ead61ba31fd5dee47c9)
![Three groups of 2 eggs. Each group of eggs has a circle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a92331bdf10c5fda0ec2f7055f3a9b9a01bc4ee2)
![Three groups of 3 eggs. Each group of eggs has a circle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/441b04efea9fd46743b581fbfef5faa0bc0970ba)

assessment_items.question_content:

hints:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or 

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$

We have a $\greenD{\text{total}}$ of $\greenD{14}$ hens.

The $\blueD{\text{number of groups}}$ of hens is $\blueD7$.  

Which image shows $\greenD{14}$ hens divided  evenly in $\blueD{7}$ groups?

This image represents the situation:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Paulo has $27$ apples divided evenly in $3$ bags.

**Which image could represent this situation?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or 

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$

We have a $\greenD{\text{total}}$ of $\greenD{27}$ apples.

The $\blueD{\text{number of groups}}$ of apples is $\blueD3$.  

Which image shows $\greenD{27}$ apples divided  evenly in $\blueD{3}$ groups?

This image represents the situation:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![Three groups of 3 apples. Each group of apples has a circle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95e85d3b8ad47a4d66b6a4180a27cf41e4481f28)

![Four groups of 9 apples. Each group of apples has a circle around it.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1e2b965b70fa4562c92cb728ace8220046ea69c3)

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ of elephants is $\maroonD7$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Avery plants $12$ flowers in her garden.  She plants $3$ flowers in each flower pot.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or 

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$

We have a $\greenD{\text{total}}$ of $\greenD{12}$ flowers.

The $\maroonD{\text{size of the groups}}$ of flowers is $\maroonD3$.  

Which image shows $\greenD{12}$ flowers divided evenly into groups of $\maroonD3$?

This image represents the situation:

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

There are $8$ bananas divided evenly into $2$ bunches.


**Which expression will tell us how many bananas are in each bunch?**

[[☃ radio 7]]
hints: There are $\greenD{8}$ bananas total.
There are $\blueD{2}$ equal bunches of bananas.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{8}\div\blueD{2}$ will tell us how many bananas are in each bunch.

options.alt: Two groups of 4 bananas. Each group of bananas has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $12$ eggs divided evenly in $3$ baskets.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression will tell us how many eggs are in each basket?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $\greenD{12}$ eggs total.
There are $\blueD{3}$ equal groups of eggs.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{12}\div\blueD{3}$ will tell us how many eggs are in the basket.

options.alt: Three groups of 4 eggs. Each group of eggs has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $40$ hot dogs divided evenly into $4$ packs.

[[☃ image 1]]


**Which expression will tell us how many hot dogs are in each pack?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $\greenD{40}$ hot dogs total.
There are $\blueD{4}$ equal packs of hot dogs.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{40}\div\blueD{4}$ will tell us how many hot dogs are in a pack.

options.alt: Four groups of 10 hot dogs. Each group of hot dogs has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kat walks $30$ dogs. She walks $5$ dogs at a time.

[[☃ image 1]]




**Which expression will tell us how many groups of dogs Kat walks?**


[[☃ radio 2]]
hints: There are $\greenD{30}$ dogs total.
There are $\maroonD{5}$ dogs in each group.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{30}\div\maroonD{5}$ will tell us how many groups of dogs Kat has to walk.

options.alt: Six groups of 5 dogs. Each group of dogs has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $10$ cookies divided evenly into groups of $2$ cookies.

[[☃ image 1]]


**Which expression will tell us how many equal groups of cookies there are?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: There are $\greenD{10}$ cookies total.
There are $\maroonD{2}$ cookies in each group.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{10}\div\maroonD{2}$ will tell us how many equal groups of cookies there are.

options.alt: Five groups of 2 cookies. Each group of cookies has a circle around it.

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

There are $63$ milk cartons. Each box holds $7$ milk cartons.


**Which expression will tell us how many boxes of milk cartons there are?**


[[☃ radio 3]]
hints: There are $\greenD{63}$ milk cartons total.
There are $\maroonD{7}$ milk cartons in each group.

We can think of division as the following:

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\blueD{\text{ Number of Groups}}$

or

$\greenD{\text{ Total}}\div\maroonD{\text{ Size of the Groups}}$
The expression $\greenD{63}\div\maroonD{7}$ will tell us how many boxes of milk there are.

options.alt: Nine groups of 7 milk cartons. Each group of milk cartons has a circle around it.
